title: triticum vulgare subsp. turgidum
intro: Triticum vulgare subsp. turgidum, commonly known as durum wheat, is a subspecies of wheat that is widely cultivated for its hard, high-protein grains. The medicinal properties of durum wheat include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. The health benefits of durum wheat include its ability to support digestive health, lower cholesterol levels, and provide essential nutrients such as fiber, iron, and B vitamins. Durum wheat can be prepared in various herbal preparations, including flour, semolina, and pasta, which can be used to make a variety of traditional dishes and products.
